concerns.text: FR-005 requires post-hoc pairwise comparisons (Tukey HSD) specifically between the three discrete noise *bins* (Low, Moderate, High). Task T024 implements a generic Tukey HSD function but lacks explicit logic to ensure it operates on the binned categorical variable derived in FR-002, risking a statistical mismatch where the test compares continuous values instead of the required categories.

FR-008 mandates multiple-comparison correction for >1 hypothesis test. Task T025 implements the correction logic but does not explicitly define the scope of hypotheses to correct (e.g., the 3 pairwise bin comparisons from FR-005 vs. the likelihood-ratio test from FR-004), creating a risk of applying correction to the wrong set of p-values.

SC-001 requires measuring the proportion of retained participants against a target of ≥85% of the recruited sample. Tasks T015 and T019 implement filtering and schema validation, but no task exists to calculate this specific retention metric, compare it to the 85% threshold, and report it as evidence for the success criterion.

SC-005 requires measuring the model convergence rate against a ≥95% success threshold. Task T027 flags warnings if >5% fail but does not explicitly calculate the convergence rate, aggregate it across primary and sensitivity sweeps, and verify it meets the 95% threshold required by the success criterion.

SC-002 requires reporting the effect size (Cohen's f²) and 95% confidence interval for the quadratic term. Task T023 performs the likelihood-ratio test, but no task explicitly extracts and formats these specific metrics (f² and CI) as the measurable evidence required by SC-002.
